FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Morgan Stanley clears digital asset trust hurdle while Fed independence survives Trump challenge; cannabis banking and CRA relief measures advance.

bullets.text: Morgan Stanley gets OCC conditional approval for digital-asset trust charter, must maintain $50M tier 1 capital and seek regulator sign-off on material business changes.
Supreme Court blocks Trump from unilaterally removing Fed Governor Lisa Cook in 5-4 decision, preserving Fed independence despite expanding presidential removal powers elsewhere.
Cannabis banking reform reintroduced following DEA's rescheduling of medical cannabis to Schedule III, opening path for financial services access.
Feds released distressed/underserved nonmetropolitan middle-income geography list, triggering CRA credit and capital relief incentives for targeted lenders.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: RIA consolidation accelerates with EP Wealth's $900M Opes deal, while Vanguard makes a stunning pivot outsourcing active funds to T. Rowe Price—signaling broader shifts in asset management strategy.

bullets.text: EP Wealth paid $900M for Opes Wealth Management, continuing aggressive RIA roll-up consolidation while bolstering talent retention via cash incentive programs for staff.
Vanguard outsourced three underperforming active funds to T. Rowe Price as subadvisor—a rare pivot away from decades of in-house management, accepting higher fees for performance.
Rep. Maxine Waters pushed back on DOL's proposed alternatives safe harbor rule, arguing it exposes retirees to illiquid assets that even institutional investors are dumping.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Insurance M&A and capital raises dominate, while cyber loss ratios deteriorate and cat reinsurance markets show signs of relief.

bullets.text: Flexpoint Ford closes $460M+ continuation fund for SageSure cat MGU, backed by Lexington Partners, Barings, and Round2—continuation vehicles remain hot for specialty insurance platforms.
US cyber insurance loss ratios hit 53% in 2025, marking second straight year of deterioration and signaling margin compression across the sector despite flat premium growth.
Kin's reciprocals secured $1.9B nat cat reinsurance at 25% cost reduction at June 1 renewal, reflecting improved market conditions and operational leverage for digital players.

Carbon Underwriting lands growth equity from FTV Capital to expand Lloyd's delegated authority platform; Lemonade reduces quota share cession to 18%, retaining more underwriting profit while adding cat capacity.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Digital banking distress, stablecoin momentum, and structural headwinds in credit markets—plus a signal on tokenized debt infrastructure.

bullets.text: Atom Bank's £600M sale is collapsing after failing to attract bids, marking a painful exit for the digital challenger that once epitomized fintech promise.
Visa, Mastercard, US Bank, Google, and Coinbase backing Open USD stablecoin signals major incumbents are moving aggressively into tokenized payments infrastructure.
Non-listed BDCs are clogging private debt fundraising as questions emerge about the real addressable market for secondaries—structural headwinds, not cyclical.
EIB's first DLT-native commercial paper issuance on Clearstream's D7 platform validates institutional appetite for blockchain-based debt infrastructure.

PE & Deals.lede: LP sentiment shifting as mega-funds chase niche strategies; Colorado PERA signals broader PE allocation pullback amid underperformance.

bullets.text: $75B Colorado PERA reconsidering PE allocation after lagging benchmarks—Aon flags this as part of a wider LP retreat from the asset class.
Generation IM closes $1B long-hold PE fund, betting on multi-decade ownership cycles in underserved segments as traditional fundraising cools.
Partners Group commits $250M as sole lead in Avenue Capital's aviation leasing continuation vehicle—specialty assets drawing institutional capital.

Markets & Macro.lede: Comcast unlocks $15–20B in M&A potential via NBCUniversal spinoff; Supreme Court ruling upends financial regulation; Fed guidance loses predictive power; BoE flags AI trading risks.

bullets.text: Comcast spinning off NBCUniversal could unleash $15–20B in deal flow as the broadband and media units separately hunt acquisitions and partnerships.
Supreme Court decision expands presidential control over SEC, CFTC, OCC but preserves Fed independence—creating regulatory whiplash risk across financial services.
Fed communication has become less predictive of actual policy moves as markets overshoot on guidance, adding noise to macro positioning.
BoE official warns AI-powered trading could amplify volatility through algorithmic herding and may require regulatory 'kill switches' to prevent systemic risk.

Newsletters.lede: 5 stories spanning new product flows, crypto headwinds, FX dislocations, advisor channel shifts, and alternatives appetite.

bullets.text: Autocallable ETFs hit $2.5B AUM in first year by simplifying a traditionally opaque, illiquid structured products market—potential distribution upside for platforms.
Bitcoin ETFs posting record outflows as crypto sentiment falters—question is whether this is cyclical pullback or structural loss of retail conviction.
JPY hits 40-year lows on Fed rate hike bets, breaking its safe-haven crown and creating FX volatility for currency traders and international portfolios.
Advisors are ditching compliance overhead by moving to Substack to build direct client relationships, signaling friction in traditional firm distribution models.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: AI economics paradox dominates: costs plummet but spending soars; structural forces reshaping enterprise outsourcing and biotech pricing models.

bullets.text: Enterprise AI spending accelerating despite 600x cost declines—scale economics and willingness-to-pay driving expansion, not unit economics. Implications for AI capex intensity and margin structures.
OpenAI's $122B valuation hinges on compute scarcity and pricing power, with Sarah Friar isolating three structural forces reshaping AI business models—critical for infrastructure investment thesis.
AI will simultaneously increase and decrease outsourcing: routine work commoditizes offshore while high-stakes decisions centralize in-house, fragmenting traditional op-ex models.

all.lede: Mixed signals across FIG: structured products gaining traction, Morgan Stanley clears crypto trust hurdle, but fintech valuations collapsing and PE doubling down on insurance.

bullets.text: Autocallable ETFs hit $2.5B AUM in year one by bringing retail access to previously illiquid, high-friction structured products—democratization working.
Morgan Stanley gets conditional OCC approval for digital-asset trust charter, clearing regulatory path for crypto services with $50M capital backstop required.
Atom Bank's sale process imploding—digital darling can't fetch £600M asking price, signaling fintech valuation reality check in tough exit market.
EP Wealth lands $900M SV RIA acquisition; PE continues aggressive RIA consolidation play while doubling down on insurance via $460M+ SageSure continuation fund.
